items.title: Secure area with one exit left hospital staff trapped by armed patient

children.content: No-one was injured during the incident at Canberra Hospital but staff were stuck inside a "secure staff station" for three hours while police disarmed a man with a knife.

image.altText: A sign that says "Emergency Building 5'' and "Main entry'' pointing to a building.

items.title: Aussie rugby may benefit from copying NZ's lateral thinking approach

children.content: Thinking outside the square has previously served Australian rugby well, which is why there are lessons it can take away from at least one of New Zealand's teams in Super Rugby Pacific.

image.altText: Leicester Fainga'anuku is tackled during the round 11 Super Rugby match between Crusaders and NSW Waratahs.

items.title: How beer and AI are changing the course of Canberra's economic future

children.content: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

gallery.altText: A man stands and smiles, leaning on a bench in a large workshop space with buckets and gear behind him.

items.title: 'Carry the hope': Thousands gather for Anzac Day service in Canberra

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs are among those to gather in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

image.altText: The facade of the Australian War Memorial was lit up before dawn on Anzac Day 2026.

items.title: Want to buy a football team? Why this club's next chapter is unwritten

children.content: Canberra United is once again without an owner and facing an uncertain future, in a tale all too familiar for players and fans of the A-League's only stand-alone women's team.

image.altText: Soccer players wearing green, black and white uniforms smile at each other during a game

items.title: 'Not what the community wanted': Big Splash lease spared despite backlash

children.content: ACT planning authorities have decided they will not follow through with a threat to terminate the lease of Big Splash water park, saying they are now satisfied the operators have made moves to reopen later this year.

image.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.

items.title: Canberrans with disability fear NDIS changes will cut vital support

children.content: Canberrans living with disability warn planned changes to the NDIS could strip back support, undermining their mental health and sense of certainty.

items.title: 'Self-belief, respect': Indigenous boxers building strength with David Pocock

children.content: A Canberra Indigenous-led boxing camp is helping young fighters hone their skills for life inside and outside the ring in its first development camp, continuing a proud tradition of First Nations involvement in the sport.

items.title: Four US presidents have been assassinated but attempts on Trump 'unusual'

children.content: The latest attack on US President Donald Trump has once again thrust his country's long history of political violence into the spotlight.

articles.title: Affidavit reveals more details as man charged with attempted Trump assassination

children.content: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

articles.title: Donald and Melania Trump slam Jimmy Kimmel for 'widow' joke

items.title: They were the first victims of the Port Arthur massacre. Their family has never forgotten

children.content: Seascape guesthouse owners Sally and David Martin were the first people murdered at Port Arthur in 1996. On the 30th anniversary of the tragedy, their granddaughter tells of how the family has refused to be defined by it.

image.altText: A man and woman pose for a photo in front of a waterfall.

items.title: The soy sauce fish is a perfect design — except on one important point

children.content: Shoyu-tai, the single-use plastic soy sauce fish that accompany takeaway sushi, are an eco-nightmare — but a local designer has a solution.

items.title: 'System ... is broken': Mounjaro won't be listed on PBS in 'stand-off' with US maker

items.title: Electric ferry needs a lift as transport ship stuck in Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Taylor's $800m pitch to double fuel reserves to come at a cost to drivers

children.content: Australia would have 60 days' worth of fuel supplies in reserve under an $800 million Coalition proposal that would cost drivers one cent per litre.
